title: Transport characteristics of bulk and edge states in an off-diagonal Aubry--André--Harper chain
abstract: We investigate quantum transport in an off-diagonal Aubry--André--Harper chain. The periodic hopping modulation generates effective internal boundaries that strongly influence the transmission characteristics. We show that edge, in-band bulk, and band-edge bulk states can be clearly distinguished through their transport signatures. In particular, bulk states near the band edges exhibit behavior similar to edge states, with weak dependence on system size, whereas in-band bulk states display pronounced size-dependent oscillations. We further demonstrate that the chain--electrode coupling strength controls the broadening of transmission resonances and drives a crossover from tunneling-dominated to nearly ballistic transport. In addition, dephasing introduces distinct sensitivity across different state classes, depending on their degree of spatial localization. These results highlight the key role of internal boundaries and quantum coherence in governing transport in modulated one-dimensional systems.
summary: This paper examines quantum transport in an off-diagonal Aubry-Andre-Harper chain, focusing on how hopping modulation creates internal boundaries. It distinguishes between the transport behaviors of edge, band-edge, and in-band bulk states. The researchers show that while edge states are robust against system size and dephasing, in-band bulk states are highly sensitive to interference and decoherence. The results demonstrate that electrode configuration and coupling strength can drive a crossover from tunneling to ballistic transport. These findings are applicable to photonic arrays, ultracold atoms, and semiconductor nanowires.
main_problem: Investigating how periodic hopping modulation in an off-diagonal Aubry-Andre-Harper chain creates internal boundaries that influence the transport signatures of edge, band-edge, and in-band bulk states.
main_result: The study identifies distinct transport signatures for different state classes, showing that in-band bulk states exhibit strong size-dependent oscillations and dephasing sensitivity, while edge and band-edge states are more robust.
method: Non-equilibrium Green's function (NEGF) method for transport, Landauer formula for current, Participation Ratio (PR) for localization, and the Buttiker probe approach for modeling dephasing.
model_or_system: A one-dimensional, commensurate off-diagonal Aubry-Andre-Harper (AAH) chain with periodic hopping modulation $t_i = t [1 + v \cos(2\pi b i + \phi)]$ connected to two semi-infinite 1D electrodes.
key_observables: Transmission probability $T(E)$, output current $I(V)$, energy spectrum, Participation Ratio (PR), and dephasing-induced peak broadening.
important_parameters: Modulation strength $v$, AAH phase $\phi$, system size $N$, electrode coupling strength, and dephasing strength.
main_assumptions: Non-interacting electron approximation, zero temperature, and a linear voltage drop across the chain for dephasing probes.
figures_summary: Fig 1: Energy spectrum and PR vs system size; Fig 3: Transmission spectra showing smooth vs oscillating peaks; Fig 5: Current vs system size for different state classes; Fig 6: PR vs modulation strength; Fig 8: Transmission spectra under varying dephasing strengths.
paper_structure: Introduction to the AAH model and transport problem; Model description and methodology; Analysis of energy spectrum and localization; Investigation of transport signatures and electrode positioning; Study of system size and coupling effects; Analysis of dephasing and decoherence; Conclusion.
why_interesting: The paper explores the interplay between quasiperiodic modulation, localization, and environmental decoherence, which is highly relevant to understanding transport in open quantum systems and the stability of localized states in many-body dynamics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 2: (Color online). Transmission probability as a function of energy for a 60-site chain. The lead–chain coupling strength is set to 1 eV. (a) Ordered chain without AAH disorder, where the source and drain electrodes are connected to sites 1 and 60, respectively. (b)–(e) Transmission spectra for the chain in the presence of oﬀ-diagonal AAH disorder with modulation strength v = 1, b = 1/4, and phase φ = 0, for diﬀerent source and drain electrode positions along the chain.

FIG. 3: (Color online). Transmission spectra for a chain of length N = 60 at φ = π/6. Bulk states are extended, with edge states at ±0.517638 eV and ±1.50597 eV. Panels (a)–(e) show transmission spectra for diﬀerent source and drain po- sitions located either in the same segment or in diﬀerent seg- ments separated by internal boundaries.

FIG. 4: (Color online). Transmission spectra for a chain of length N = 60 for diﬀerent chain–electrode coupling strengths. Red, green, blue, orange, and cyan curves cor- respond to 0.2 eV, 0.6 eV, 1.0 eV, 1.4 eV, and 1.8 eV, respec- tively. The parameters are v = 1.0 and b = 1/4. (a) Bulk- state transport for φ = π/4 with electrodes connected to sites 1 and 60 (NS = 1, ND = 60). The inset shows an enlarged view near E = 0. (b) Edge-state transport for φ = π/2 with both electrodes connected to site 1 (NS = ND = 1).

FIG. 5: (Color online). Output current as a function of sys- tem size N for bulk and edge states at diﬀerent Fermi-energy positions. The red curve corresponds to bulk states within the band interior, obtained by setting EF = 0 with φ = π/4 and connecting the electrodes to the two terminal sites of the chain. The blue curve represents bulk states near the band edge, for EF = 2.41875 eV with φ = π/4 and the elec- trodes connected to sites 4 and 5. The green curve corre- sponds to edge states, obtained by setting the Fermi energy at the ﬂat-band energy E = 0 with φ = π/2. All calculations are performed at zero temperature with an applied bias volt- age V = 1 V and chain–electrode coupling...

FIG. 6: (Color online). Participation ratio (PR) of the zero- energy state as a function of the AAH modulation strength v for b = 1/4 and odd system sizes N = 21, 41, and 61 (red, green, and blue curves, respectively). The nonmonotonic vari- ation of the PR, including a local maximum near v ≈1.415, reﬂects a crossover from extended to edge-localized behavior, followed by size-independent localization at larger v.

FIG. 7: (Color online). Density plots of the transmission probability as a function of energy E and AAH phase φ for a chain of length N = 20. (a) Edge-state transport with both electrodes connected to the ﬁrst site. (b) In-band bulk trans- port with electrodes attached to the ﬁrst and Nth sites. (c) Band-edge bulk transport with electrodes connected to neigh- boring interior sites (fourth and ﬁfth sites). The qualitative features are robust against changes in system size, with iden- tical edge-state structures for systems within the same family deﬁned by N = 4m + r (r = 0, 1, 2, 3).

FIG. 8: (Color online). Transmission probability as a func- tion of energy E for diﬀerent dephasing strengths in an oﬀ- diagonal AAH chain of length N = 60, where B¨uttiker probes are attached to each lattice site. The red, green, blue, orange, and cyan curves correspond to dephasing strengths of 0, 0.3, 0.6, 0.9, and 1.2,eV, respectively. (a) Edge-state transport with NS = ND = 1 and φ = π/2, showing weak sensitivity to small dephasing and gradual peak suppression at larger de- phasing strengths. (b) Band-edge bulk-state transport with NS = 4, ND = 5, and φ = π/4, exhibiting moderate suppres- sion and peak broadening. (c) In-band bulk-state transport with NS = 1, ND = 60, and φ = π/4,...
